questions.question: Explain the concept of framing in the Data Link Layer. Why is it necessary?
keyPoints: Framing is dividing bit stream into manageable data units called frames
Enables receiver to identify start and end of each data unit
Provides error detection capability through FCS
Handles addressing (source and destination)
Ensures reliable data transfer between adjacent nodes

questions.question: Differentiate between character-oriented and bit-oriented framing with examples.
keyPoints: Character-oriented uses special characters (DLE STX, DLE ETX) for boundaries
Bit-oriented uses flag pattern (01111110) for boundaries
Character-oriented limited to character sets (ASCII)
Bit-oriented works with any bit pattern
Examples: BSC uses character-oriented, HDLC/PPP use bit-oriented

questions.question: Explain the bit stuffing and destuffing process with an example.
keyPoints: Insert '0' after five consecutive '1' bits in data
Prevents flag pattern (01111110) from appearing in data
At receiver, remove '0' after five '1' bits to recover original data
Ensures transparent data transfer

questions.question: What are the three types of HDLC frames? Briefly describe each.
keyPoints: I-frames (Information frames): Carry user data with sequence numbers
S-frames (Supervisory frames): Used for flow and error control (RR, RNR, REJ)
U-frames (Unnumbered frames): Handle link establishment/termination (SABM, DISC, UA)
I-frames contain both send and receive sequence numbers
S-frames do not contain information field

questions.question: Describe the structure of a PPP frame with its various fields.
keyPoints: Flag field: 01111110 (0x7E) marks start and end
Address field: 0xFF (always broadcast)
Control field: 0x03 (no link control)
Protocol field: Indicates protocol of encapsulated data (IP, IPCP, etc.)
Information field: Contains the payload data (0-1500 bytes)
FCS field: 2-4 bytes for error detection

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Frame Check Sequence (FCS)? How does it work?
keyPoints: FCS provides error detection capability
Usually implements CRC (Cyclic Redundancy Check)
Sender computes CRC over frame contents and appends to trailer
Receiver recomputes CRC and compares with received FCS
Detects burst errors and corrupted frames
If errors detected, frame is discarded

questions.question: Explain the byte-count framing method with its advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Uses a length field in header to specify number of bytes in frame
Receiver counts bytes to determine frame boundaries
Advantage: No need for escape characters in data
Disadvantage: Length field can be corrupted causing synchronization loss
Example: DDCMP protocol uses byte-counting
Complex error recovery required if count is wrong
